title: Exploring the impact of seasonal variation on the toxicological profile of Σ2PFAS in surface water bacterioplankton communities.
author_string: Li, Yiping, Ibrahim, Muhammad, Yue, Cheng, Wang, Yaning, Hongzhe, Pan et al.
abstract: There is little information available about the freshwater bacterioplankton ecology in relation to PFAS exposure. This is the very first instance that Illumina high-throughput sequencing has been used to thoroughly examine the composition and structure of the microbial community upon interaction with emerging contaminants, specifically PFOA and PFOS (Σ2PFAS) during flood season (FS) and off-flood season (OFS) in the lentic freshwaters of a subtropical monsoon ecosystem. The following mean concentrations of PFOA (10.34 ± 1.25 and 10.47 ± 0.46) and PFOS (7.72 ± 0.93 ng L<sup>-1</sup> and 7.59 ± 0.61 ng L<sup>-1</sup>) were observed during FS and OFS, respectively. The findings indicate a statistically non-significant seasonal change (P < 0.05) in the level of Σ2PFAS between the two seasons. For the richness of bacterioplankton community, the average Chao 1 was 1609.78 during the FS, significantly higher (P < 0.01) than that observed during the OFS. Proteobacteria account for over 80 % of the samples; however, their abundance significantly reduced during the OFS. RDA results revealed that during the FS, the PFOA, PFOS, and ORP were positively correlated with each other, and were responsible for controlling the abundance of bacterioplankton community at sampling locations WS3, WS5, WS7, and WS9. However, during the OFS, the Σ2PFAS were positively associated with the concentration of COD, and were responsible for shaping the community dynamics of Cyanobacteria and Gemmatimonadota at WS4, WS5, WS7, and WS8. The findings of this study suggest that the dominant bacterioplankton should be lab-investigated (microcosm) for a synergistic role in the PFAS transformation and degradation. By understanding the relationship between seasons and pollution, the present study enables predictive models and early-warning systems to proactively address PFAS damage in the lentic freshwaters of a subtropical monsoon ecosystem.
